opps.title: INTEGRATED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES
opps.description:
The Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC) seeks contractor support to enhance recruiting operations through program management, analytical services, and advanced information technology. A key focus of this requirement is the development, deployment, and sustainment of custom business applications using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI). The contractor will deliver business process automation solutions, including Robotic Process Automation (RPA), to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and support data-driven decision-making.

Additionally, the contractor will provide graphic design and content creation services to support both internal and external communications. This includes designing user interfaces for applications, developing marketing materials, and producing visually engaging content for outreach and stakeholder engagement. All content must align with Coast Guard branding and approval protocols.

Other services include planning and analysis led by Project Management Professional (PMP) certified personnel, performance management, strategic planning, meeting facilitation, documentation, process improvement, change coordination, reporting, and knowledge management. Personnel must have relevant certifications and government experience.

The period of performance is one base year plus four option years. Work will primarily occur at Coast Guard Headquarters in Washington DC, with possible off-site work as approved. All contractor staff must meet pre-employment screening and background investigation requirements.

efficiency of mission-specific processes.

Technical expertise shall be applied collaboratively with government personnel to ensure alignment with strategic objectives, compliance standards, and emerging AI governance frameworks. The contractor is required to provide their own laptops, peripherals, and internet/Wi-Fi access for the duration of the contract.

All interested parties must review the attached PWS to understand the full scope of the contract.

opps.title: Marketing Support
opps.description:
This procurement is for marketing support services in accordance with the request for quotation (RFQ) and the statement of work (SOW). Supplier quotations will be provided in two separate documents. Quotation documents must be compatible with Adobe Acrobat, Microsoft Word and Excel (Pricing Matrix). Documents must not exceed 5 megabytes in total file size.

The two parts, as separate documents, are: 
1) Technical Quotation 
1a) A brief explanation (maximum of 5 pages) of offeror's ability to satisfy the statement of work and prior experience. 
2) Pricing Matrix (attached to this solicitation) must be completed and returned as an Excel spreadsheet.

In order to document compliance with FAR 52.204-7(b)(1), your quotation response must include your organization's unique entity identifier (UEI) from www.SAM.gov.

The Government intends to evaluate quotations and award without discussions with offerors, but reserves the right to conduct discussions if the Contracting Officer determines them to be necessary. The offeror's initial proposal should contain the offeror's best price and delivery, plus include a complete but concise technical quotation.

This requirement is a small business set-aside.  NAICS 541613, size standard $19.0 million applies to this requirement.

Award will be made via Best Value Trade Off.  Award may be made to other than the lowest priced offer.  Award may be made to other than the best technically evaluated offer.

Please provide your quotation by 5-15-2026 at 3 PM MST.

[End of Quotation Instructions]

Deliver training to regional staff, ensuring readiness and effective execution. Establish mechanisms to capture return on investment (ROI) through program monitoring, and manage technical assistance for Cybersecurity Advisors and State Coordinators. Support annual program monitoring and grant close-out activities.

2. opps.title: Program Management Office Support
opps.description:
This requirement is for contractor support to provide three Agile-driven teams to deliver program management, risk management, and knowledge management services for the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP) systems and programs. The contractor will support performance measurement, process improvement, data analytics, requirements management, and business case development for SEVP operations. Responsibilities include developing and maintaining risk management plans for the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) Programs Section, SEVP Business Automation Section, and System Management Unit. The contractor support team will coordinate risk reviews, provide independent risk assessments, and manage risk inventories and mitigation strategies.

Additionally, the contractor team will support the operational management of program documentation and knowledge libraries within the SEVP Assessment Management System (SEVPAMS), ensuring proper version control and metadata management. Strategic planning and administrative support will be provided to facilitate efficient operations, compliance with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) privacy and information assurance policies, and continuous improvement of SEVP systems and processes. The scope excludes software development and focuses on management, administration, and support functions.

The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Digital Infrastructure Services Center (DISC) must decommission our enterprise data center (EDC) in Kansas City, Missouri. USDA DISC will need to securely move physical IT equipment and other support equipment between DISC or customer sites. Due to scheduling and security requirements, not all equipment can be transported through commercial freight options to meet our needs. DISC is looking for support in transporting equipment securely and safely between USDA/DISC and customer sites. This work will include ensuring data integrity is maintained, transporting equipment within an agreed timeline, ensuring all transported equipment is received at the destination in the same condition as the departure facility.  All equipment shall be visually inspected and documented at the point of origin, with photographs and/or written condition reports by the designated DISC representative.  Upon delivery, the receiving DISC representative will use these documents to confirm equipment condition.

opps.description:
The 87th Contracting Squadron is seeking information from industry for the purpose of conducting market research for VMware Cloud Foundation software licensing. This notice does not obligate the Government to award a contract or any other type of contractual document.

THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) NOTICE ONLY. THIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTATIONS (RFQ). NO SOLICITATION IS AVAILABLE AT THIS TIME.

Statement of Characteristics

The 87th Communications Squadron requires enterprise-grade virtualization software licensing to support its IT infrastructure. The requested VMware Cloud Foundation software licensing is intended to sustain and modernize critical environment functionality, support mission readiness, and maintain operational efficiency.

Specifications

The Government is seeking sources capable of providing VMware Cloud Foundation software licenses and standard manufacturer support. This requirement is strictly for software and licensing only. No hardware installation, site readiness validation, physical decommissioning services, or on-site labor are required.

The anticipated requirement includes VMware Cloud Foundation annual subscription licensing for a base year, with optional additional licensing periods for Option Years 1 through 4. The contractor shall provide the exact software part numbers and quantities identified in the Statement of Work, to include VMware Cloud Foundation annual subscription licensing and any required external ordering fee.

The requested licensing must include a standard manufacturer software subscription and support coverage for the duration of the active license term. Support shall include access to all software updates, security patches, and remote technical support provided by the vendor. The primary deliverable is proof of entitlement and license keys, provided electronically to the designated Government Point of Contact upon award.

The anticipated periods of performance are as follows:

Base Year: 02/01/2026 &ndash

Background
 The 87th Communications Squadron (87th CS) is seeking to procure enterprise-grade virtualization software licensing, specifically VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF), to support its IT infrastructure.
